title: [MCP test] Add success marker file to confirm Jira‚Üín8n‚ÜíCursor flow
instructions:
1) From the project root (the same folder that contains package.json or README), create a new file named either MCP_TEST_SUCCESS.txt or MCP_TEST_SUCCESS.md. 2) Put exactly the following text into the file (no additional characters, no extra whitespace, no extra lines):

Changes performed successfully.

3) Save the file as UTF-8 without a BOM. 4) Stage and commit only this new file. Example git commands:
   - git checkout -b chore/mcp-test-success
   - git add MCP_TEST_SUCCESS.txt   (or MCP_TEST_SUCCESS.md)
   - git commit -m "MCP test: add success marker"
   - git push --set-upstream origin chore/mcp-test-success
5) If your workflow requires it, open a pull request so the change is visible to CI/integration that triggers the Jira ‚Üí n8n ‚Üí Cursor flow. Do not modify any other files or code.
acceptance_criteria: A file named MCP_TEST_SUCCESS.txt or MCP_TEST_SUCCESS.md exists in the project root (the same folder as package.json or README).
The file content is exactly: Changes performed successfully. (no extra characters, no extra whitespace, no extra newline).
The file is saved with UTF-8 encoding (no BOM).
